posts.text:
Wow, looks like Strava is going the enshittification route: "... we care deeply about developers, and the health of the developer ecosystem" meanwhile they will now be requiring a subscription for any API access. The framing is anti-AI-scraping, but the practical effect is that casual free API access is ending.

As a longtime Strava user, this is disappointing to see hostility towards the dev ecosystem.

https://communityhub.strava.com/insider-journal-9/an-update-to-our-developer-program-13428

Released Rackula v26.5.0 and you might be wondering "hey, wasn't it on like v0.10??" - and you're right! we just switched to Calendar Versioning (CalVer), so I don't have to overthink between a major, minor or patch release. Also, because biggest number is best number.

Also includes some prep work for an upcoming Proxmox Community Scripts LXC deployment method.

Changelog: https://github.com/RackulaLives/Rackula/releases/tag/v26.5.0
